changes_made: Removed k8s_etcd_no_unencrypted_static_authenticators as it overlaps with k8s_etcd_encryption_at_rest_enabled
review_notes: The control is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves ensuring that secrets are not stored in an unencrypted form. The functions mostly align with K8s security practices, but some naming and redundancy issues needed addressing. The function for etcd was redundant as encryption at rest covers the requirement. The naming for external secret management was adjusted for consistency with existing patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names are consistent with existing patterns and avoid redundancy
title: Authenticator Management | No Embedded Unencrypted Static Authenticators
step1_reasoning: The control 'IA-5 (7) - No Embedded Unencrypted Static Authenticators' focuses on ensuring that static authenticators (like passwords or tokens) are not stored in an unencrypted form. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that Secrets are not stored in plain text and are encrypted at rest, particularly in etcd. Additionally, using external secret management solutions can help manage and rotate secrets securely.
